# @platform/infra-aws

AWS infrastructure definitions: ECS/EKS workloads, RDS, ElastiCache, S3, IAM, and network topology.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/infra-aws
Version: 0.1.0
Component: infra-aws
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: delivery
Bounded context: aws-infra
Owner: team-platform
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: tooling
Class: active.tooling
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-platform
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: false
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Define Terraform configurations for all AWS resources used by the platform
- Manage network topology (VPC, subnets, security groups) per environment
- Provision compute workloads (ECS tasks or EKS deployments) for application services
- Define IAM roles, policies, and instance profiles following least-privilege principle

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not own Terraform workflow tooling or module patterns (tooling-terraform)
- Does not define application container images (tooling-docker)
- Does not manage DNS or CDN configuration

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Applied via tooling-terraform scripts as part of the infrastructure pipeline in tooling-ci
- Per-environment workspaces isolate state between development, staging, and production
